name: Viking 1
first: First fully successful Mars landing; first long-duration surface science
description: The mission that gave us our first sustained look at the Martian surface. Viking 1's lander operated for 6 years 4 months at Chryse Planitia — running biology experiments designed to detect life (results ambiguous; one experiment produced a positive signal still debated decades later), measuring weather, returning panoramic images, and analysing soil composition. The orbiter mapped 97% of Mars at 100m resolution and helped scout landing sites for missions still decades away.

events.label: LAUNCH
events.note: Titan IIIE-Centaur from Cape Canaveral LC-41.

events.label: MARS ORBIT INSERTION
events.note: Orbiter captures into Mars orbit; lander remains attached for site survey.

events.label: LANDER SEPARATION
events.note: Lander detaches for descent to Chryse Planitia.

events.label: TOUCHDOWN
events.note: Lander touches down 1976-07-20, exactly 7 years after Apollo 11.
